title: 5 teens arrested after Panera brawl; BPD has responded to location more than 15 times this year, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Five teenagers arrested for allegedly assaulting police and causing a disturbance at Panera Bread in Dorchester.
Charges include trespassing, assault and battery on a police officer, and resisting arrest for three of the teens.
Boston police have responded to more than 15 incidents involving teenagers at this Panera location since January.

title: ‘I am scared for my safety’: Plymouth police officer placed on leave is accused of sexual abuse, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine and husband Daniel Forand accused of sexually assaulting their legal ward.
Pelrine placed on paid administrative leave on March 17 due to the criminal investigation into the allegations.
Court hearings for Pelrine and Forand are scheduled for March 27 in Plymouth District Court.

title: Testimony of officer who shot Kelsey Fitzsimmons continues on day 2 of trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Officer Patrick Noonan, who shot Fitzsimmons, testified about the June 30, 2025 incident during the bench trial.
Judge Jeffrey Karp allowed limited questioning on Noonan's conduct post-shooting, including being drug tested and working while on leave.

title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Mackenzie Roy, a Natick native, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming autoimmune diseases.
Roy is participating in the marathon to support B.A.A. Gives Back, promoting community, health, and fitness.
Roy grew up watching the Boston Marathon as a family tradition and has aspired to run it for 15 years.

title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens begins his professional hockey career with the Providence Bruins after signing an AHL amateur tryout agreement.
The 19-year-old forward, drafted seventh overall in 2025, will debut against the Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday evening.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel emphasizes the importance of adjusting to AHL competition for Hagens' development and future in Boston.

title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' coach Joe Mazzulla and player Derrick White enjoyed WWE Monday Night Raw at TD Garden with their families.
Mazzulla, an MMA fan, spoke about the importance of learning from different experiences, including pro wrestling events.
White and Mazzulla highlighted the value of off-court relationship-building, drawing parallels between sports and wrestling.

title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Comella's closed its Melrose location on March 20 after 13 years due to operational and financial challenges.
The Melrose restaurant at 478A Main St. was independently owned and operated by a franchisee.
Comella's, founded by Gene Comella in 1988, continues operating 10 other Massachusetts locations.

title: The most underrated movies of 2025 are streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The most underrated movies of 2025 include 'The Naked Gun', 'The Ballad of Wallis Island', and '28 Years Later'.
'The Naked Gun' stars Liam Neeson and Pamela Anderson and is praised for its humor and joke-filled script.
'The Ballad of Wallis Island' features Tim Key and Carey Mulligan as a comedic duo; it's available on Prime Video.

title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 22-year-old man was fatally shot by Westminster police after allegedly threatening them with a knife.
The police were serving an arrest warrant at the man's home on South Ashburnham Road around 4:40 p.m.
The officers involved are on administrative leave while State Police and Westminster police investigate the shooting.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch's reaction on 'Celebrity Jeopardy! All Stars' went viral after a fortuitous clue about the Spanish Armada.
Dratch discussed the viral moment on 'Late Night with Seth Meyers' while promoting her Broadway role in 'The Rocky Horror Picture Show.'
Dratch credited her celebrity hairdresser, Eddie Cook, for inadvertently prepping her for a key 'Jeopardy!' question.
